general_info.definition: strong fluorescent carbon dots based on the pyrolysis of xylose and m-phenylenediamine(mPD)

method.conditions: Precursors dissolving in 10 mL of ultrapure water, then mixed with: (200mL of H3PO4 for 2-CDs and 3-CDs), (200mL of HNO3 and CH3COOH for 4-CDs and 5-CDs), (absence of acid for 6-CDs), (0.50g of Na3PO4 and 0.37g of NaOH for 7-CDs and 8-CDs); Heated in the microwave reactor at: 220°C (1-CDs, 4-CDs, 5-CDs, 6-CDs, 7-CDs, 8-CDs), 200°C (2-CDs), 180°C (3-CDs) for 10 min

post_treatment: Filtration through 0.22 μm PTFE syringe filter
Dialysis using 500 Da cut off membrane for 48 h

structural.morphology: (quasi-spherical shape and excellent dispersibility for 1-CDs to 4-CDs), (irregular for 5-CDs to 8-CDs)
structural.crystallinity: Crystalline for 1-CDs to 4-CDs with lattice spacing of 0.34 nm corresponding to graphite (002) plane; amorphous for 5-CDs to 8-CDs

surface_groups: N-H(blue-CDs have almost no stretching vibration)

applications.domain: Fluorescent ink for anti-counterfeit Printing and Information Encryption
applications.performance: 1-CDs ink shows green emission at pH 3-7 and cyan emission at pH 8-11 under UV light (365 nm). Printed patterns (QR codes, text) exhibit reversible fluorescence switching when treated with dilute NaOH (blue shift) and CH₃COOH (recovery to green). High stability: negligible photobleaching after 300 min UV irradiation, thermal stability at 0ºC up to 200°C, and chemical stability against common bleachers (ethanol, methanol, soda lime).
applications.comparative_advantage: Renewable precursor (xylose), facile one-step synthesis, pH-tunable dual emission for advanced security features, and excellent stability compared to conventional fluorescent inks.
